subject: Statistics
title: When to Use Which Distribution
description: A 60-second lesson on When to Use Which Distribution.

hook.text: Counts of rare events? Poisson. Time between failures? Exponential. Pass/fail? Binomial.

buildup.text: Choosing the right distribution depends on what your data measures and how it was generated.

discovery.text: Ask: is it discrete or continuous? Bounded or open-ended? Symmetric or skewed?

twist.text: The wrong distribution gives confident but wrong answers. The math works — the model is just wrong.

climax.text: No test can tell you which distribution is 'correct.' Only which one fits your data best.

punchline.text: Right model, right answers. Wrong model, confident lies.

quiz.question: What happens if you choose the wrong distribution?
options: You get mathematically valid but meaningless results
The calculation always fails with an error
The answer is always close enough
